description: Combining ibuprofen and aspirin can increase the risk of gastrointestinal ulcers and bleeding. It may also decrease the cardioprotective effect of low-dose aspirin.
advice: Consult your doctor before using these together. Monitor for signs of bleeding.

description: Concomitant use of warfarin and aspirin significantly increases the risk of serious bleeding events.
advice: Avoid combination unless explicitly directed and closely monitored by a physician. Seek immediate medical attention if unusual bleeding or bruising occurs.

description: NSAIDs like ibuprofen can cause stomach ulcers and bleeding, and they also thin the blood, dramatically increasing the risk of severe bleeding when taken with warfarin.
advice: Do not take ibuprofen while on warfarin unless prescribed by your doctor. Use alternative pain relievers like acetaminophen if approved.

description: Nitroglycerin combined with sildenafil can cause a sudden, severe, and life-threatening drop in blood pressure.
advice: NEVER use sildenafil if you are taking nitroglycerin or other nitrates. Seek emergency medical services immediately if chest pain occurs.

description: Lisinopril is an ACE inhibitor that can increase potassium levels in your blood. Taking it with potassium supplements can lead to dangerously high potassium levels (hyperkalemia).
advice: Monitor blood potassium levels. Do not take potassium supplements or use salt substitutes containing potassium without consulting your healthcare provider.
